channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Wolverine + Marten: My random takes and updates
item.description: Some time has passed since my last love-hate post about this stack. Short update: I hate to say it, but cough — I like it. A while back I wrote a post called "Wolverine + Marten: My story and...

item.title: Dart for Java Developers
item.description: Dart for Java Developers A deep, practical mapping from Java to Dart: syntax, type system, concurrency, tooling, Flutter, and backend (Shelf, Relic, Serverpod, and the broader pub.dev ecosystem)....

item.title: Distributed Shopify Inventory Sync: Architecture Guide for Scale
item.description: Keeping inventory accurate across Shopify, warehouses, and marketplaces sounds simple. At scale, it is one of the hardest engineering problems in ecommerce. A single API call after each sale works...

item.title: Hister: The Most Privacy-Respecting Search Engine
item.description: Privacy and web search are in constant tension. Every time you type a query into a search engine you are handing over something valuable: a window into what you are thinking. Most people accept...

item.title: Gemma 4, Read My Ingredient Label and Tell Me If It’s Lying: A Personal AI Health Filter
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 *Most apps still treat “healthy” like it’s a universal setting. High protein? Great. Low fat? Great. Organic? Great. * Except…...

item.title: The Bottleneck Was Never the Model — It's the Routing Layer
item.description: In May 2026, a widely-discussed essay on Hacker News argued that "the bottleneck was never the code" — AI code generation has solved the coding bottleneck, but the real bottlenecks remain in...

item.title: Top 5 Social Media APIs for Developers in 2026
item.description: Most developers don't quit because the idea was wrong. They quit because they spent three weeks wiring up six different OAuth flows, handling rate limits from five separate platforms, and...

item.title: The Three Bottlenecks AI Can't Code Away
item.description: TL;DR AI writes nearly half of all new code, but teams aren't shipping faster. The bottleneck moved from writing code to coordination, governance, and review. GraphQL Federation addresses all...

item.title: Building SwiftDeploy: A Self-Writing Infrastructure Tool with OPA Policy Enforcement and Prometheus Observability
item.description: Building SwiftDeploy: A Self-Writing Infrastructure Tool with OPA Policy Enforcement and Prometheus Observability Introduction What if your deployment tool could refuse to deploy when your disk is...

item.title: From Gulf Coast Pipe Racks to AI Agent Orchestration: The Engineer's Path
item.description: I spent six years designing pipe racks and equipment platforms for Gulf Coast oil and gas facilities. Now I also build AI agent systems that run six businesses. These two things are not as...

item.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
item.description: Dozens of US states are considering legislation to allow people to install plug-in solar systems, often called balcony solar. These small arrays require little to no setup and could help cut...

item.title: PyPI Packages Deliver ZiChatBot Malware via Zulip APIs on Windows and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered three packages on the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository that are designed to stealthily deliver a previously unknown malware family called ZiChatBot...

item.title: What’s next for IVF
item.description: Forty-eight years ago this July, Louise Joy Brown became the world’s first person born with the help of in vitro fertilization. Millions more IVF babies have entered the world since then. And...

item.title: Five architects of the AI economy explain where the wheels are coming off
item.description: Earlier this week, five people who touch every layer of the AI supply chain sat down at the Milken Global Conference in Beverly Hills, where they talked with TechCrunch about everything from chip...

item.title: vm2 Node.js Library Vulnerabilities Enable Sandbox Escape and Arbitrary Code Execution
item.description: A dozen critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in the vm2 Node.js library that could be exploited by bad actors to break out of the sandbox and execute arbitrary code on susceptible...

item.title: Musk’s biggest loyalist became his biggest liability
item.description: I sat down in the Musk v. Altman trial courtroom today, painfully aware that no one was going to ask Shivon Zilis the question on everyone's minds: Girl, what the fuck are you doing? Zilis, who...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Last-Ditch Effort to Control OpenAI: Recruit Sam Altman to Tesla
item.description: Messages between Shivon Zilis and Tesla executives reveal plans in 2017 to start a rival AI lab, potentially led by Altman or Demis Hassabis.

item.title: Nintendo announces a new Star Fox for the Switch 2
item.description: It turns out Fox McCloud's appearance in the Super Mario Galaxy Movie was a tease of things to come: Nintendo just surprise announced the first new Star Fox game in a decade. The game is called,...

item.title: Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But ‘trust is irrelevant’ as AGI nears, he says.
item.description: Barry Diller defended OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, while warning that AGI remains an unpredictable force needing guardrails.

item.title: Snap says its $400M deal with Perplexity ‘amicably ended’
item.description: The deal, announced last November, would have seen Perplexity's AI search engine integrated directly into Snapchat.

item.title: Is xAI a neocloud now?
item.description: xAI's real business may be more about building data centers than training AI models.

item.title: Google shuts down Project Mariner
item.description: Google has pulled the plug on Project Mariner, an experimental feature designed to perform tasks for you across the web, as reported earlier by Wired's Maxwell Zeff. The Project Mariner landing...

item.title: Mirai-Based xlabs_v1 Botnet Exploits ADB to Hijack IoT Devices for DDoS Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have exposed a new Mirai-derived botnet that self-identifies as xlabs_v1 and targets internet-exposed devices running Android Debug Bridge (ADB) to enlist them in a...

item.title: How David Sacks crashed and burned in the White House
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter exclusively for Verge subscribers about tech, politics, and Washington intrigue. (It's basically House of Cards, but for nerds.) Not a subscriber yet?...

item.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
The latest Tile Pro is down to $25 — its best price of the year
item.description: If you’re struggling to find a Mother’s Day gift under $30, location trackers are a good investment, helping mom keep tabs on keys, bags, luggage, and other valuables. The latest Tile Pro is one...

item.title: Anthropic Gets in Bed With SpaceX as the AI Race Turns Weird
item.description: In an unexpected turn, the two companies signed a deal for Anthropic to use computing resources from Elon Musk’s xAI.

item.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
item.description: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

item.title: What we’re hearing about the new homepage
item.description: We’ve been reading your feedback on the new homepage. All of it. The kind and encouraging comments, the brutal ones, the “actually this rules,” the “please undo this immediately,” the bug reports,...

item.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
item.description: Mira Murati, OpenAI's former CTO, has testified under oath that CEO Sam Altman lied to her about the safety standards for a new AI model. In a video deposition shown during the ongoing Musk v....
